title: Symplocos puberula
intro: Symplocos puberula, also known as the hairy syringa, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the tropical regions of Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral activities, which can help to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Symplocos puberula include syringin, a unique glycoside, as well as other phenolic compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often made into various herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including respiratory and gastrointestinal disorders.
taxonomy: Symplocos puberula is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Symplocaceae. This family is a small group of flowering plants commonly known as the sweetleaf or alderleaf family. The genus Symplocos, to which S. puberula belongs, is native to the tropical and subtropical regions of the world. The plant is an evergreen shrub or small tree, and its classification is within the order Ericales. Symplocos puberula is native to Mexico and Central America.
